channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Chatlectify: turn your chat history into a writing style your LLM can reuse
item.description: Two years of daily Claude + ChatGPT. They've seen probably a million tokens of my writing. Every response still opens with "Certainly!" or "Great question!" and closes with "In conclusion…"....

item.title: How to Add Authentication to Any App in Under an Hour (2026 Guide)
item.description: Auth is the part every tutorial skips. Here's a complete, honest breakdown of your three real options in 2026. Authentication is where most "build an app in a weekend" tutorials quietly stop. They...

item.title: Mastering the SQL Analytical Toolkit
item.description: As a budding data professional, I recently completed an intensive SQL Week 2 Assignment that pushed me from basic queries into more powerful territory. I worked with two real-world databases —...

item.title: How to Optimize React & Next.js Apps for SEO and Rank Faster
item.description: I spent 3 hours debugging why Google couldn't see my React app. Turns out, Googlebot was rendering a blank white page — because my entire content lived inside client-side JavaScript that hadn't...

item.title: Introducing KyberSwap MCP
item.description: Introducing KyberSwap MCP: Connecting AI Agents to Real DeFi Execution This article introduces KyberSwap MCP, a Model Context Protocol server designed to connect AI agents and developer workflows...

item.title: Easiest OpenClaw Setup & Onboarding Guide + Using Web Search
item.description: This is a submission for the OpenClaw Writing Challenge If you've been looking into autonomous Node.js frameworks lately, you've probably stumbled across OpenClaw. It’s incredibly powerful, but...

item.title: Multi region kubernetes cluster (Onprem/Public Cloud)
item.description: A single Kubernetes cluster is manageable. You deploy things, they run, life is good. Things get complicated when you have clusters in multiple regions that need to talk to each other. This post...

item.title: Your AI Sounds Most Confident Right Before It's Wrong — Here's the Data
item.description: Let's start with something that took me a while to sit with properly. AI models are 34% more likely to use confident language — phrases like "definitely," "certainly," "without question" — when...

item.title: Zero Waste, Zero Stress: Building ReMake with AI-Assisted Github Copilot Development
item.description: 🎯 Introduction What if your discarded cardboard box could become a laptop stand? What if that old denim jacket could transform into a trendy tote bag? Second Life ReMake is an AI-powered upcycling...

item.title: 5 Data Patterns That Scream 'Your Developer Is Burning Out
item.description: Nobody quits on a Monday. The resignation email you receive on a random Thursday was written — emotionally — six weeks ago. The disengagement started three months ago. And the data saw it coming...

item.title: Vercel Breach Tied to Context AI Hack Exposes Limited Customer Credentials
item.description: Web infrastructure provider Vercel has disclosed a security breach that allows bad actors to gain unauthorized access to "certain" internal Vercel systems. The incident stemmed from the compromise...

item.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

item.title: Blue Origin successfully reused its New Glenn rocket
item.description: Today's launch of AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite aboard Blue Origin's reusable New Glenn rocket was a partial success. The New Glenn touched down on its landing pad without incident,...

item.title: Cloud development platform Vercel was hacked
item.description: Vercel, a major development platform that hosts and deploys web apps, was compromised, and the hackers are attempting to sell stolen data. A person claiming to be a member of ShinyHunters, which...

item.title: The 12-month window
item.description: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

item.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
item.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: Nothing’s modular CMF Headphone Pro are down to their lowest price to date
item.description: Over the past several years, Nothing’s budget-centric subsidiary, CMF, has built a reputation for producing attractive, inexpensive products with features often reserved for gadgets that cost...

item.title: The Stars My Destination is classic proto-cyberpunk
item.description: This might feel like a somewhat obvious recommendation to some, but it flew under my radar until now. Alfred Bester's The Stars My Destination (originally published as Tiger! Tiger! in the UK) is...

item.title: Marathon battery life makes Keychron’s Ultra 8K keyboards its best yet
item.description: Keychron's expansive keyboard catalog covers everything from basic full-size mechanical boards to niche layouts like southpaw Alice or a one-handed half-keyboard for gaming. Its Q and V series are...

item.title: The future of local TV news has taken a Trumpian turn
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more stories on Big Tech versus politics in Washington, DC, follow Tina Nguyen and read...

item.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
item.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

item.title: The RAM shortage could last years
item.description: According to Nikkei Asia, even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages...

item.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
item.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

item.title: Judge rules Trump administration violated the First Amendment in fight against ICE-tracking
item.description: Jorge L. Alonso, a federal district court judge for the Northern District of Illinois, said that the Trump Administration violated the First Amendment when it pressured Facebook and Apple to...

item.title: Cheap stuff that doesn’t suck, take 3
item.description: From fancy OLED TVs to robot vacuums with arms, we tend to cover a lot of cool stuff here at The Verge that, unfortunately, often costs as much as a month’s rent (or more). But with the ongoing...

item.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
item.description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

item.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
item.description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

item.title: Dyson’s handheld fan is more powerful and louder than I expected
item.description: Two years ago I attended a picturesque outdoor wedding in August where the hot and humid weather had guests occasionally ducking into their cars to enjoy a blast of AC. Dyson’s new $99.99 handheld...

item.title: It Takes 2 Minutes to Hack the EU’s New Age-Verification App
item.description: Plus: Major data breaches at a gym chain and hotel giant, a disruptive DDoS attack against Bluesky, dubious ICE hires, and more.
